skill.name: reasoningbank-adaptive-learning-with-agentdb
skill.description: Implement adaptive learning workflows with ReasoningBank and AgentDB, including trajectory tracking, verdict scoring, memory distillation, and pattern reuse for self-improving agents.
skill.summary: Build adaptive learning loops for agents with ReasoningBank and AgentDB.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command patterns, network URLs, and weak cryptography indicators. Review found the command patterns are markdown code fences or a documented npm quick start, the URLs are documentation links, and the weak cryptography hits are false positives on ordinary text rather than cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: Documented Package Install Command
low_findings.description: README.md contains a quick-start npm install and npx command. This is not executed by the skill, but users who run it would execute third-party package code and should verify the package source.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is plainly inside a markdown quick-start block and is not an automatic execution path. It still has low supply-chain relevance if a user follows the instructions.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: PROCESS.md and SKILL.md link to external ReasoningBank and AgentDB documentation sites. No evidence found that the skill fetches these URLs or sends data to them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network indicators are simple documentation links with no request code. The residual concern is only that users may leave the marketplace to visit external sites.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution patterns are markdown code-fence delimiters around TypeScript examples. The reported weak cryptography indicators do not correspond to any cryptographic operation in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines show markdown fences, TypeScript snippets, and role labels rather than shell backticks or crypto APIs. No evidence found of command injection, hashing, encryption, or malware intent.

content.user_title: Build Adaptive Learning Agents with AgentDB
content.value_statement: Agent teams need a repeatable way to learn from decisions and outcomes. This skill provides a structured ReasoningBank workflow for tracking trajectories, scoring verdicts, distilling patterns, and applying learned strategies.
actual_capabilities: Defines a five-phase adaptive learning workflow for ReasoningBank and AgentDB.
Shows how to initialize an AgentDB-backed ReasoningBank instance.
Explains trajectory tracking for decisions, reasoning paths, context, and outcomes.
Describes verdict judging with criteria such as efficiency and correctness.
Outlines memory distillation and pattern application for future decisions.
limitations: Does not include a complete runnable application or test suite.
Assumes users already understand AgentDB and reinforcement learning concepts.
Uses illustrative TypeScript snippets that may need adaptation for real APIs.
References external documentation sites without verifying their availability.

use_cases.title: Prototype Self-Learning Agents
use_cases.description: Create a structured loop that records decisions, evaluates outcomes, and reuses successful patterns.

use_cases.title: Design Agent Evaluation Pipelines
use_cases.description: Plan how to score reasoning trajectories against measurable criteria before applying learned behavior.

use_cases.title: Document Adaptive Learning Architecture
use_cases.description: Turn ReasoningBank concepts into implementation phases, components, metrics, and review checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Plan the Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline a five-phase ReasoningBank and AgentDB implementation plan for my agent project.

prompt_templates.title: Define Trajectory Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design the trajectory records, outcome fields, and verdict criteria for my adaptive agent.

prompt_templates.title: Create Learning Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to define success metrics for trajectory accuracy, verdict quality, pattern learning, and decision improvement.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my ReasoningBank architecture and identify gaps in tracking, judging, distillation, and pattern reuse.

output_examples.input: Plan a ReasoningBank implementation for a support triage agent.
output_examples.output: A phased implementation plan covering database setup, trajectory capture, verdict scoring, pattern distillation, and improved routing decisions.

output_examples.input: Define verdict criteria for an agent that recommends actions.
output: Efficiency score based on time and resource use.
Correctness score based on outcome quality.
Novelty score based on useful new strategies.
Improvement score compared with baseline decisions.

output_examples.input: Review whether my adaptive learning loop is complete.
output_examples.output: A gap analysis that checks initialization, tracked context, outcome feedback, pattern consolidation, and measurement of future decision quality.

best_practices: Define verdict criteria before collecting trajectories.
Track context, reasoning, action, and outcome together.
Measure learned behavior against a fixed baseline.
anti_patterns: Do not store trajectories without outcome feedback.
Do not apply learned patterns without quality thresholds.
Do not treat illustrative snippets as production-ready code.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps structure an adaptive learning loop for agents using ReasoningBank and AgentDB concepts.

faq.question: Does it include production code?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance and illustrative TypeScript snippets that require project-specific implementation.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What prior knowledge is useful?
faq.answer: Users should understand AgentDB, reinforcement learning concepts, and basic neural network workflows.

faq.question: Can it evaluate agent decisions?
faq.answer: It describes verdict judgment using criteria such as efficiency, correctness, and novelty.

faq.question: Does it contact external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found of automatic network requests. The files only include external documentation links.
